name: Thermostat Climate Controller
description: The `thermostat` climate platform allows you to control a climate control system in much the same manner as a physical thermostat. Its operation is similar to the [Bang-Bang](https://esphome.io/components/climate/bang_bang/) controller; a sensor measures a value (the air temperature) and the controller will try to keep this value within a range defined by the set point(s). To do this, the controller can activate devices like a heating unit and/or a cooling unit to change the value observed by the sensor. When configured for both heating and cooling, it is essentially two [Bang-Bang](https://esphome.io/components/climate/bang_bang/) controllers in one; it differs, however, in that interaction with the thermostat component is nearly identical to that of a real thermostat.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the climate device. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the climate device in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Cool Action
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `cool_action`, `dry_action`, `fan_only_action`, `heat_action`.

The action to call when the climate device should enter cooling mode to decrease the current temperature.

config_entries.label: Dry Action
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `cool_action`, `dry_action`, `fan_only_action`, `heat_action`.

The action to call when the climate device should perform its drying (dehumidification) action. The thermostat controller does not trigger this action; it is invoked by `dry_mode` (see below).

config_entries.label: Fan Only Action
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `cool_action`, `dry_action`, `fan_only_action`, `heat_action`.

The action to call when the climate device should activate its fan only (but does not heat or cool). When `fan_only_cooling` is set to `false`, the thermostat controller immediately triggers this action when set to `fan_only_mode`  ; however, when `fan_only_cooling` is set to `true`, this action is called based on the upper target temperature (similar to `cool_action` above).

config_entries.label: Heat Action
config_entries.description:
**Required — set at least one of:** `cool_action`, `dry_action`, `fan_only_action`, `heat_action`.

The action to call when the climate device should enter heating mode to increase the current temperature.

config_entries.label: Humidity Sensor
config_entries.description: If specified, this sensor is used to measure the current humidity. This may be used for humidity control; see [Humidity Control Actions](https://esphome.io/components/climate/thermostat#humidity-control-actions).

config_entries.label: Idle Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device should enter its idle state (not cooling, not heating).

config_entries.label: Min Idle Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration the idle action must be active before calling another climate action.

config_entries.label: Sensor
config_entries.description: The sensor that is used to measure the current temperature.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Action State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish climate device action changes to.

config_entries.label: Auto Mode
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into "auto" mode (it may both cool and heat as required). This mode is different from `heat_cool_mode` (above) in that it takes control of the set points away from the user; it's generally intended for exclusive use by automations.

config_entries.label: Cool Deadband
config_entries.description: The minimum temperature differential (temperature above the set point) before calling the cooling [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions). Defaults to 0.5 °C.

config_entries.label: Cool Mode
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into cool mode (it may cool as required, but not heat).

config_entries.label: Cool Overrun
config_entries.description: The minimum temperature differential (cooling beyond the set point) before calling the idle [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions). Defaults to 0.5 °C.

config_entries.label: Current Humidity State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish current humidity changes to.

config_entries.label: Current Temperature State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish current temperature changes to.

config_entries.label: Default Preset
config_entries.description: The name of the preset to use by default. Must match a preset as per [preset](https://esphome.io/components/climate/thermostat#thermostat-preset).

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Dry Mode
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into dry mode (for dehumidification).

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Auto Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should be set to "auto" mode (the fan is controlled by the climate control system as required).

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive fan mode commands on.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Diffuse Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should direct its airflow over a broad area.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Focus Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should direct its airflow at a specific area.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode High Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should run at its maximum speed.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Low Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should run at its minimum speed.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Medium Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should run at an intermediate speed.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Middle Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should direct its airflow at an intermediate area.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Off Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should never run.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode On Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should run continuously.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode Quiet Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should run at quiet speed.

config_entries.label: Fan Mode State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish fan mode changes to.

config_entries.label: Fan Only Action Uses Fan Mode Timer
config_entries.description: If set to `true`, the `fan_only_action` will share the same delay timer used for all `fan_mode` actions. The minimum fan switching delay is then determined by `min_fan_mode_switching_time` (see below). This is useful when `fan_only_action` controls the same physical fan as the `fan_mode` actions, common in forced-air HVAC systems.

config_entries.label: Fan Only Cooling
config_entries.description: If set to `true`, when in the `fan_only_mode` climate mode, the `fan_only_action` will only be called when the observed temperature exceeds the upper set point plus `cool_deadband`. When set to `false` (the default), `fan_only_action` is called immediately when `fan_only_mode` is activated, regardless of the current temperature or set points. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Fan Only Mode
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into fan-only mode (it may not heat or cool, but will activate its fan either immediately or, when `fan_only_cooling` is `true`, as needed based on the upper target temperature value).

config_entries.label: Fan With Cooling
config_entries.description: If set to `true`, `fan_only_action` will be called whenever `cool_action` is called. This is useful for forced-air systems where the fan typically runs with cooling. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Fan With Heating
config_entries.description: If set to `true`, `fan_only_action` will be called whenever `heat_action` is called. This is useful for forced-air systems where the fan typically runs with heating. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Heat Cool Mode
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into "heat/cool" mode (it may both cool and heat as required). If no action is desired, may be set to `true` to enable the mode without a related automation. Note that **both** `heat_action` **and** `cool_action` ([see above](https://esphome.io/components/climate/thermostat#heating-and-cooling-actions)) must be defined to enable this mode.

config_entries.label: Heat Deadband
config_entries.description: The minimum temperature differential (temperature below the set point) before calling the heating [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions). Defaults to 0.5 °C.

config_entries.label: Heat Mode
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into heat mode (it may heat as required, but not cool).

config_entries.label: Heat Overrun
config_entries.description: The minimum temperature differential (heating beyond the set point) before calling the idle [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions). Defaults to 0.5 °C.

config_entries.label: Humidity Control Dehumidify Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when dehumidification is required.

config_entries.label: Humidity Control Humidify Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when humidification is required.

config_entries.label: Humidity Control Off Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when (de)humidification should stop. This action is **required** when either of the above actions are configured.

config_entries.label: Humidity Hysteresis
config_entries.description: The maximum humidity differential (above/below the set point) before calling the respective humidity control [action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#config-action). Defaults to 1%.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Max Cooling Run Time
config_entries.description: Duration after which `supplemental_cooling_action` will be called when cooling is active. Note that `supplemental_cooling_action` will be called repeatedly at an interval defined by this parameter, as well, enabling multiple stages of supplemental (auxiliary/emergency) cooling.

config_entries.label: Max Heating Run Time
config_entries.description: Duration after which `supplemental_heating_action` will be called when heating is active. Note that `supplemental_heating_action` will be called repeatedly at an interval defined by this parameter, as well, enabling multiple stages of supplemental (auxiliary/emergency) heating.

config_entries.label: Min Cooling Off Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration the cooling action must be disengaged before it may be engaged.

config_entries.label: Min Cooling Run Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration the cooling action must be engaged before it may be disengaged.

config_entries.label: Min Fan Mode Switching Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration any given fan mode must be active before it may be changed.

config_entries.label: Min Fanning Off Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration the fanning action must be disengaged before it may be engaged.

config_entries.label: Min Fanning Run Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration the fanning action must be engaged before it may be disengaged.

config_entries.label: Min Heating Off Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration the heating action must be disengaged before it may be engaged.

config_entries.label: Min Heating Run Time
config_entries.description: Minimum duration the heating action must be engaged before it may be disengaged.

config_entries.label: Mode Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive climate device mode commands on.

config_entries.label: Mode State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish climate device mode changes to.

config_entries.label: Off Mode
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device is placed into "off" mode (it is completely disabled).

config_entries.label: Preset
config_entries.description: (*Optional*, list)

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: Name of the preset. If this is one of the *standard* presets (`eco`, `away`, `boost`, `comfort`, `home`, `sleep`, or `activity`  ) it is considered a *standard* preset. Any other string will make the preset a *custom* preset. *Standard* and *custom* presets are functionally equivalent, the only difference is that when switching the mode via [climate.control Action](https://esphome.io/components/climate#climate-control-action) you will need to use the `preset` or `custom_preset` property as appropriate. The Home Assistant `climate.set_preset_mode` service treats them identically

config_entries.label: Default Target Temperature High
config_entries.description: The default high target temperature when switching to this preset.

config_entries.label: Default Target Temperature Low
config_entries.description: The default low target temperature when switching to this preset

config_entries.label: Fan Mode
config_entries.description: The fan mode the thermostat should switch to when this preset is activated. If not specified, the thermostat's fan mode will remain unchanged when the preset is activated. One of:

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: The mode the thermostat should switch to when this preset is activated. If not specified, the thermostat's mode will remain unchanged when the preset is activated. One of:

config_entries.label: Swing Mode
config_entries.description: The fan swing mode the thermostat should switch to when this preset is activated. If not specified, the thermostat's fan swing mode will remain unchanged when the preset is activated. One of:

config_entries.label: Preset Change
config_entries.description: (*Optional*, [Action](https://esphome.io/automations/actions#all-actions)): The action to call when the preset is changed. This will be called either when a user changes the mode through the Home Assistant UI or through a call to `climate.control`

config_entries.label: Preset Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive preset commands on.

config_entries.label: Preset State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish preset changes to.

config_entries.label: Set Point Minimum Differential
config_entries.description: For dual-point/dual-function systems, the minimum required temperature difference between the heat and cool set points. Defaults to 0.5 °C.

config_entries.label: Startup Delay
config_entries.description: If set to `true`, when ESPHome starts, `min_cooling_off_time`, `min_fanning_off_time`, and `min_heating_off_time` must elapse before each respective action may be invoked. This option provides a way to prevent damage to equipment (for example) disrupted by a power interruption. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Supplemental Cooling Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device should activate supplemental cooling to (more aggressively) decrease the current temperature. *This action is called repeatedly at an interval defined by* `max_cooling_run_time` *(see below).*

config_entries.label: Supplemental Cooling Delta
config_entries.description: When the temperature difference between the upper set point and the current temperature exceeds this value, `supplemental_cooling_action` will be called immediately.

config_entries.label: Supplemental Heating Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the climate device should activate supplemental heating to (more aggressively) increase the current temperature. *This action is called repeatedly at an interval defined by* `max_heating_run_time` *(see below).*

config_entries.label: Supplemental Heating Delta
config_entries.description: When the temperature difference between the lower set point and the current temperature exceeds this value, `supplemental_heating_action` will be called immediately.

config_entries.label: Swing Both Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should oscillate in horizontal and vertical directions.

config_entries.label: Swing Horizontal Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should oscillate in a horizontal direction.

config_entries.label: Swing Mode Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive swing mode commands on.

config_entries.label: Swing Mode State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish swing mode changes to.

config_entries.label: Swing Off Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should remain in a stationary position.

config_entries.label: Swing Vertical Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the fan should oscillate in a vertical direction.

config_entries.label: Target Humidity Change Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the thermostat's target humidity is changed.

config_entries.label: Target Humidity Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive target humidity commands on.

config_entries.label: Target Humidity State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish target humidity changes to.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature Change Action
config_entries.description: The action to call when the thermostat's target temperature(s) is/are changed.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive target temperature commands on.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature High Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive higher target temperature commands on.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature High State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish higher target temperature changes to.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature Low Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive lower target temperature commands on.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature Low State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish lower target temperature changes to.

config_entries.label: Target Temperature State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish target temperature changes to.

config_entries.label: Visual
config_entries.description: Visual settings for the climate device - these do not affect operation and are solely for controlling how the climate device shows up in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Max Humidity
config_entries.description: The maximum humidity the climate device can reach. Used to set the range of the frontend gauge.

config_entries.label: Max Temperature
config_entries.description: The maximum temperature the climate device can reach. Used to set the range of the frontend gauge.

config_entries.label: Min Humidity
config_entries.description: The minimum humidity the climate device can reach. Used to set the range of the frontend gauge.

config_entries.label: Min Temperature
config_entries.description: The minimum temperature the climate device can reach. Used to set the range of the frontend gauge.

config_entries.label: Temperature Step
config_entries.description: The granularity with which the target temperature can be controlled. Can be a single number, or split as below:
